title: `podman image load` on MacOS fails with `Error: unable to load image: payload does not match any of the supported image formats`
body:
### Issue Description

When trying to load an image with `podman image load` the operation fails with `Error: unable to load image: payload does not match any of the supported image formats` even though the image is valid.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Try to load the image with `TMPDIR=/private/tmp podman image load <~/Downloads/cEOSarm-lab-4.34.4M.tar.xz`


### Describe the results you received

```shell
~/Downloads ▶ TMPDIR=/private/tmp podman image load <~/Downloads/cEOSarm-lab-4.34.4M.tar.xz
Error: unable to load image: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: open /var/tmp/libpod-images-load.tar1381150876/index.json: not a directory
 * oci-archive: creating temp directory: untarring file "/var/tmp/container_images_oci3444481229": operation not permitted
 * docker-archive: loading tar component "manifest.json": file does not exist
 * dir: open /var/tmp/libpod-images-load.tar1381150876/manifest.json: not a directory
[Exit 125]
```

### Describe the results you expected

The image should be loaded.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I tried explicitly setting the `TMPDIR` environment variable because I saw it in another issue but it doesn't seem to take?

comments.body: not sure how to proceed here ... you say the image is valid, podman says no,.  how do we tiebreak this?  can you attach the image?
Could you also load your image with Docker? That might give some indication as to where this is heading.

Hello,

you are absolutely right, the problems is with the image. I tried it with docker and it didn't load there as well. Sorry for the noise. In the future I will test with docker first.

The image is proprietary so I couldn't share it.
